mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 2.79% suggests room for further easing
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth accelerates above a 150,000 person monthly threshold, signaling robust labor demand and consumer spending capacity that drives positive equity returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong labor demand
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following PAYEMS acceleration above 150k are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales and housing starts signals weakening consumer demand and construction activity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline month-over-month simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS at 344,895 million dollars, HOUST at 1,837 thousand units
regime_hint: demand-side slowdown or early recession signal
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.035 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production declines below its 3-month moving average, indicating supply-side weakness and reduced capacity utilization that constrains profit margins and drives negative equity returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following INDPRO declines below 3-month MA are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises above 5.5%, signaling labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations decline.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 5.2%, near trigger threshold
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early-stage recession
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following UNRATE > 5.5% are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth decelerates below 2% annualized, combined with rising inflation (CPIAUCSL > 3% year-over-year), signals stagflationary pressure that compresses equity valuations and produces negative forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth < 2% annualized AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year change > 3%
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 11,078.2 billion (2017 dollars), CPIAUCSL at 193.2
regime_hint: stagflationary environment with conflicting monetary policy constraints
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of stagflationary episodes, or Rank IC < 0.045 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When federal funds rate exceeds 4.0%, monetary tightening dampens credit availability and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days unless simultaneously accompanied by accelerating payroll growth above 200,000 persons monthly.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.0% AND PAYEMS monthly change < 200,000 persons
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 2.79%, below trigger threshold
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with weakening labor dynamics
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in this regime are positive in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.035 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts accelerate above 1,900 thousand units annualized, signaling strong residential investment demand and forward-looking economic optimism that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST at 1,837 thousand units, near trigger threshold
regime_hint: housing-led expansion with strong residential demand
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following HOUST > 1,900 are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
